components.title: Collector, Evaluator, and Reporting Pipeline
components.description: Headless pipeline that collects, normalizes, evaluates, assesses, signs, and publishes sanitized assurance artifacts.
components.purpose: Maintain immutable control evidence and assessment lifecycle records independent of the browser interface.

control-implementations.description: Capabilities implemented by the assurance plane.

implemented-requirements.description: Identity and access evidence is collected and ownership reviews are retained as signed workpapers.
The collector has read-only assessed-scope access and write access only to its private evidence destination.
A versioned event taxonomy defines security, assurance, command, and AI tool events.
Weekly review workpapers record anomalous events, failed collections, and rejected escalations.
Daily and change-triggered jobs preserve evidence freshness and never translate missing evidence into pass.
Compiled infrastructure and immutable image digests form the approved baseline compared to live inventory.
Deployment provenance connects pull request, commit, image digest, What-If result, and Azure Activity event.
Evidence storage uses versioning, soft delete, lifecycle retention, and restore tests.
Findings create immutable risks using a documented 5x5 rubric and explicit treatment authority.
SBOM and vulnerability scan outputs are retained and critical unresolved vulnerabilities block release.
Deterministic test and contract gates must pass before release evidence is signed.
Approved KQL queries test telemetry freshness and four focused Sentinel use cases.
Reviewer decisions remain authoritative; AI suggestions cannot approve exceptions, accept risk, conclude compliance, or close findings.
